challenges.title: Carbon Offset
challenges.summary: A Kick stream capture of Clav and ASU Frat Leader hides a compressed evidence bundle after the real JPEG footer.
challenges.scenario: The photo of Clav and ASU Frat Leader looks pretty normal from the stream capture, except Clavicular got brutally frame mogged and the upload is larger than it should be. Solvers need to confirm where the real JPEG ends, carve the appended payload, and extract the hidden note to obtain the flag.
learningObjectives: Recover appended evidence from a seemingly normal media file

artifacts.name: clav_stream_capture_<seed>.jpg
artifacts.purpose: Generated JPEG based on the provided stream image with an appended archive payload

hints: A JPEG should stop after its normal footer.
Check the tail of the file instead of only viewing the image.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the generated image theme, decoy archive contents, or solver briefing while keeping the append-and-carve solve path intact.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not expose the real flag in plaintext metadata or portal frontend files.

challenges.title: Template Bleed
challenges.summary: A mall incident DOCX about food-court sabotage still leaks a hidden draft asset tied to the Jamba Juice vs. Cinnabon feud.
challenges.scenario: Kyan Kotschevar-Smead still works at Jamba Juice after leaking classified Boeing materials, and has been documenting repeated sabotage after James Richards-Perhatch from Cinnabon started poaching customers and interfering with orders. Mall management distributed a cleaned-up DOCX packet, but one embedded media object still contains a concealed draft archive from the original complaint. Solvers need to unpack the Office container, inspect the `word/media` assets, and carve the hidden payload to recover the flag.
learningObjectives: Treat Office files as ZIP containers during forensic review
Locate hidden or overlooked media inside application package structures

artifacts.name: food_court_incident_<seed>.docx
artifacts.purpose: Generated Office package containing the mall-management incident packet and a suspicious media object

artifacts.name: mall_briefing.txt
artifacts.purpose: Background note framing the food-court sabotage investigation

hints: A DOCX is a ZIP package, not a single opaque blob.
Check `word/media` before assuming mall management removed every draft artifact.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the food-court complaint narrative, embedded media naming, or hidden archive contents while preserving the package-inspection solve path.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not make the hidden evidence directly readable from the outer DOCX bytes.

challenges.title: Deceitful Transfer
challenges.summary: A parked-outside surveillance PCAP captures an FTP gallery sync tied to suspected infidelity and hidden spending.
challenges.scenario: You are a private investigator hired by a woman who believes her husband has been cheating, spending thousands on OnlyFans, and stashing pictures through plain FTP. You park outside the house long enough to capture a packet trace of his session. The hard part is that the FTP traffic contains multiple passive data streams and decoy files: solvers need to reconstruct the right transfers, identify the meaningful image, and inspect that recovered JPEG for hidden appended data.
learningObjectives: Map FTP control traffic to multiple passive-mode data channels
Correlate a recovered text artifact with a second recovered media artifact
Carve hidden appended data from a transferred JPEG

artifacts.name: deceitful_transfer_<seed>.pcap
artifacts.purpose: Generated packet capture with multiple passive-mode FTP data streams

artifacts.name: incident_memo.txt
artifacts.purpose: Case note from the private-investigator surveillance setup

hints: More than one passive data stream matters in this capture.
One recovered text file points to which image deserves a closer look.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the FTP case story, decoy transfer set, or hidden JPEG payload while keeping the multi-stream reconstruction path intact.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not put the flag directly into plaintext control-channel traffic.

challenges.title: Mailbox Heist
challenges.summary: A rogue Claude Bot fired off a 4:23 AM email chain, and the sealed compensation memo is split across multiple EML clues.
challenges.scenario: A locally hosted Claude Bot decided your Deloitte salary was an insult and, at 4:23 AM, blasted messages to recruiters, payroll, your own inbox, and a Deloitte hiring manager. No single email is enough: one message sets the recovery order, one hides a clue inside a wrapped HTML link, one buries a clue in a compressed attachment, and another carries the sealed memo. Solvers need to correlate the full message set and use the recovered phrase to decrypt the final evidence.
learningObjectives: Correlate clues across multiple related EML artifacts
Recover and decode MIME attachments and wrapped-link metadata
Use recovered clues to decrypt a final sealed artifact

artifacts.name: 0423-self-archive.eml
artifacts.purpose: Self-addressed message that sets the recovery order

artifacts.name: 0423-deloitte-recruiting.eml
artifacts.purpose: Recruiter outreach email with a clue hidden inside a wrapped HTML link

artifacts.name: 0423-payroll-dissent.eml
artifacts.purpose: Payroll complaint email with a compressed attachment

artifacts.name: 0423-hiring-manager.eml
artifacts.purpose: Hiring-manager email containing the sealed memo archive

artifacts.name: triage_note.txt
artifacts.purpose: Analyst note describing the suspicious 4:23 AM message burst

hints: Not every clue is in a visible plain-text body.
One clue is hidden in a wrapped URL and another is inside a compressed attachment.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the email chain, clue encoding, or sealed memo contents while preserving the multi-email recovery path.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not expose the attachment contents or real flag in plaintext portal assets.

challenges.title: Ledger Burn
challenges.summary: A finance ZIP is padded with a hidden compressed payload after its official end-of-directory record.
challenges.scenario: A valid ZIP archive opens normally, but the raw file length exceeds what the ZIP metadata says it should contain. Solvers must use the central directory information to locate the real archive end, carve the trailing payload, and decompress the hidden evidence.
learningObjectives: Validate archive metadata against raw file length
Carve appended content after a legitimate container footer

artifacts.name: quarter_close_<seed>.zip
artifacts.purpose: Generated archive with a hidden compressed tail

artifacts.name: audit_note.txt
artifacts.purpose: Case narrative that points to suspicious accounting activity

hints: Use archive metadata to find the real end of the ZIP.
The archive is not corrupt. Something was added after it.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the visible finance records, trailing compressed payload, or difficulty hints while keeping the EOCD carve workflow intact.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not replace the trailing payload with plaintext data.

challenges.title: Gone But Not Forgotten
challenges.summary: A disk image still contains a deleted note, and the flag is recoverable with basic filesystem forensics.
challenges.scenario: Investigators seized a laptop image after a suspect tried to remove a sensitive text file before the handoff. The visible files are mostly noise, but the deleted data is still recoverable. Solvers need to inspect the image with forensic tooling, recover the deleted note, and submit the flag it contains.
learningObjectives: Recover deleted evidence from a raw disk image
Compare quick triage with deeper filesystem analysis tools

artifacts.name: README_FIRST.txt
artifacts.purpose: Introductory case note explaining the investigation goal

artifacts.name: laptop.img
artifacts.purpose: Disk image containing a deleted text file with the flag

hints: Start with a quick strings pass before moving to heavier forensic tooling.
If you load the image in Autopsy, inspect deleted content instead of only allocated files.
Carving tools can recover content even after a file is deleted.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the full challenge definition, image asset, generator, and internal notes.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Improve the disk image realism, add decoy deleted files, or expand the solution notes while keeping the solve path centered on recovering a deleted text artifact.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not expose the flag in plaintext metadata or move challenge assets outside this folder.
